phases.name: Data Pipeline & Core Components
phases.description: This collection of repositories represents the foundational infrastructure and core components of the Nominate-AI platform. It includes processing and analysis of data through tools like quantifi and meshnet, alongside specialized modules for invoice scanning and data extraction. The development covers the building of essential systems and modules necessary for the platform's overall functionality.

milestones.title: mapr - Enhancing the Mapr Header Mapping tool

phases.name: Nominate-AI Core Development
phases.description: This repository represents the foundational development of the Nominate-AI platform, encompassing crucial infrastructure changes, documentation updates, and core feature implementation. It’s a phased evolution from initial setup to the development of key components like the Pocket Field Kit, lochness, and Mogger, focusing on building a robust and scalable system.

milestones.title: Initialize gmail-mirror project with build fix
Complete conversion documentation and batch processing tools
Develop the Mogger system, focusing on improved data parsing, error correction, and system validation.

phases.name: Data Pipeline & Infrastructure
phases.description: This repository represents the core development of the Nominate-AI platform’s data pipeline and infrastructure. It encompasses the creation of foundational components like `flock`, `larus`, `seedbed`, `clarion`, and `campainbrain`, designed to facilitate the processing and management of data for AI model training and deployment. The project focuses on building robust and scalable data handling capabilities.

phases.name: Development Pipeline
phases.description: This repository represents the core development pipeline for the Nominate-AI platform, encompassing the evolution of various components from initial concept to stable implementation. It spans a period of focused development and refinement, addressing key functionalities and ensuring a robust foundation for future enhancements and integrations.

milestones.title: cbapp - Backend Service for Data Management
system - Documentation and Tooling for Nominate.ai
cbdistricts - Python Library for CBAP Data

phases.name: Backend Infrastructure & Documentation
phases.description: This project encompasses the core infrastructure and documentation for the CBApp backend system, including the `cbsurveys`, `cbinfra`, `cbfiles`, and `cbdocs` repositories. It’s focused on enhancing the stability and maintainability of the backend, specifically through updates to the backend service for managing files and data, and improving the documentation for the CBmesh project.

phases.name: Core Infrastructure & API Development
phases.description: This repository represents the foundational development of the Nominate-AI platform’s intelligence API infrastructure, encompassing core components like `cbintel`, `cbproject`, `cbauth`, `cbos`, `cbmaintain`, `cbtest`, and `cbsms`. It’s a phased approach to ensuring stability, scalability, and maintainability of the platform's core systems.

phases.name: Core Infrastructure & Feature Development
phases.description: This repository represents the foundational development of the Nominate-AI platform, encompassing key components like the API for unstructured document processing (`cbunstruct`), the WireGuard-to-TOR tunnel infrastructure (`cbtor`), and the Vite preview enhancements for `cbmobile` (`cbmobile`). It also includes crucial components for user guides (`cbuserguide`), indexing and vectorization (`cbindex`), and caching (`CBTiles`) to improve performance and efficiency.  The development phase is heavily focused on establishing the core functionalities and ensuring stability of the platform.

milestones.title: Vite Preview Enhancements – Mobile Campaign Brain Support

phases.name: Campaign Brain Development & Infrastructure
phases.description: This repository represents the core development and maintenance of the Campaign Brain platform, encompassing its various components – from campaign brain geocoding to city map poster services and a robust management interface. It’s a continuous effort to improve the functionality, stability, and documentation of the Nominatim service and the underlying infrastructure.

milestones.title: cbgeo - Python Campaign Brain Geocoding Service
cbmaps - Python City Map Poster Service
cbcli - Python CLI for Nomination AI Management
cbcdn - JavaScript Campaign Brain Self-Hosted CDN Assets
cblinks - Python FastAPI URL Shortener Service
cbscout - Python Agent Memory and Knowledge Management Service

phases.name: Phase 9
phases.description: Development of cboverseer, cbwebhook, cbissues and 4 more repos.

phases.name: Campaign Management & Tooling
phases.description: This repository represents the core development of the Nominate-AI platform’s infrastructure and tools. It encompasses crucial components like the MCP server, cbnetwork, CB Executive Summaries, and the various tools for managing campaign data and integrations. These efforts are focused on streamlining the workflow for creating and maintaining the LLM agent catalog and supporting the platform’s functionality.

phases.name: Development & Refinement
phases.description: This repository represents a phased evolution of the Nominate-AI platform, encompassing the development and refinement of core components. It begins with the foundational `cbradio-ui` and `cbrouter` tools, followed by `cbtemplates` and `cbcron` – all focused on enhancing radio station management and scheduling capabilities.  The `cbradio-web` and `cblogs` repositories represent further refinement of existing functionalities and focused on improving the stability and usability of the platform.

phases.name: Development Pipeline
phases.description: This repository represents the core development pipeline for the Nominate-AI platform, encompassing the creation of several key components. It starts with a basic TTS service wrapper and progressively evolves to infrastructure improvements, API integrations, and specialized data wrappers, showcasing a phased approach to building and refining the platform's functionality.

The repository also includes a migration from black/flake8 to ruff, updates to documentation, and a focus on addressing security vulnerabilities and ensuring code quality.
